breaking_news.title: Google patches two actively exploited Chrome zero-days affecting Skia and V8
breaking_news.summary:
Google released emergency patches for two high-severity zero-day vulnerabilities in the Chrome browser that are actively being exploited in the wild. The flaws, tracked as CVE-2026-3909 and CVE-2026-3910, affect the Skia graphics library and the V8 JavaScript engine, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary code.

CISA has added these vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, urging immediate patching across all enterprise environments. This incident highlights the persistent threat of browser-based attacks and the critical need for rapid update management to prevent widespread compromise.

breaking_news.title: CrackArmor vulnerabilities in Linux AppArmor expose 12 million systems to root access
breaking_news.summary:
Security researchers at Qualys disclosed nine critical vulnerabilities in the Linux AppArmor security module, collectively dubbed CrackArmor. These flaws allow unprivileged users to bypass kernel protections, escalate privileges to root, and break out of container isolation without administrative credentials.

The vulnerabilities impact over 12 million enterprise Linux systems running Ubuntu, Debian, and SUSE distributions, posing a significant risk to cloud and containerized environments. Exploitation could lead to full system compromise, making immediate mitigation essential for organizations relying on AppArmor for mandatory access control.

breaking_news.title: GlassWorm supply-chain attack abuses Open VSX extensions to target developers
breaking_news.summary:
The GlassWorm threat actor has escalated its supply-chain campaign by abusing extension dependency relationships in the Open VSX registry to deliver malware. Researchers identified at least 72 malicious extensions that pose as helpful developer tools while serving as transitive delivery vehicles for a malware loader.

This sophisticated technique allows benign-appearing packages to pull in malicious dependencies after trust is established, targeting developers using Visual Studio Code. The attack compromises the integrity of the development ecosystem and risks infecting thousands of developer workstations globally.

breaking_news.title: Veeam patches critical RCE flaws in Backup & Replication platform
breaking_news.summary:
Veeam released security updates to address multiple critical vulnerabilities in its Backup & Replication platform, including three remote code execution flaws. These vulnerabilities, rated 9.9 on the CVSS scale, allow authenticated users to execute code on backup servers and manipulate files.

Compromising backup infrastructure is a primary objective for ransomware operators, as it undermines recovery capabilities and enables data destruction. Organizations using Veeam solutions must prioritize patching to prevent attackers from disabling their disaster recovery mechanisms.

breaking_news.title: Storm-2561 distributes fake VPN clients to steal enterprise credentials
breaking_news.summary:
Microsoft Defender Experts uncovered a credential-theft campaign by Storm-2561 that uses SEO poisoning to distribute fake enterprise VPN clients. The threat actor impersonates vendors like Cisco, Fortinet, and Ivanti to trick users into downloading trojanized installers that steal corporate login credentials.

This campaign targets enterprise users searching for legitimate software, leveraging trusted branding to bypass suspicion and gain access to secure networks. The stolen credentials can facilitate further lateral movement and data exfiltration within victim organizations.

breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters claims one petabyte data theft from Telus Digital
breaking_news.summary:
The extortion group ShinyHunters claims to have stolen up to one petabyte of data from Telus Digital, a major Canadian business process outsourcing firm. The breach allegedly exposed support recordings, source code, and employee records after unauthorized access to the company's systems.

Telus Digital confirmed the incident and is investigating the scope of the unauthorized access while maintaining that business operations remain fully functional. This massive data theft underscores the growing threat of ransomware groups targeting service providers to maximize impact and leverage.

breaking_news.title: German intelligence officials targeted in Signal and WhatsApp account takeover campaign
breaking_news.summary:
High-ranking German officials, including the former Vice President of the BND, were targeted in a sophisticated account takeover campaign against Signal and WhatsApp users. Attackers posed as support staff to trick victims into revealing their PINs, facilitating unauthorized access to encrypted messaging accounts.

This espionage campaign highlights the vulnerability of even secure communication platforms to social engineering attacks against high-value targets. The incident raises concerns about the security of government officials and the potential for intelligence leaks through compromised personal devices.

breaking_news.title: AppsFlyer Web SDK hijacked to spread crypto-stealing JavaScript code
breaking_news.summary:
The AppsFlyer Web SDK was temporarily hijacked in a supply-chain attack that injected malicious JavaScript code designed to steal cryptocurrency. The compromise allowed attackers to distribute crypto-stealing payloads to websites integrating the popular analytics SDK.

This incident demonstrates the risks associated with third-party dependencies in web development and the potential for widespread impact from a single compromised library. Developers are urged to audit their SDK integrations and monitor for unauthorized code injections.

breaking_news.title: FBI launches inquiry into Steam games spreading malware to gamers
breaking_news.summary:
The FBI is seeking victims of a malware campaign that distributed malicious games through the Steam platform between May 2024 and January 2026. Investigators identified eight specific titles containing malware and are asking gamers who installed them to come forward for assistance.

This investigation highlights the growing trend of threat actors using legitimate gaming platforms to distribute malware to unsuspecting users. The scale of the campaign suggests a significant number of potential victims across the gaming community.

breaking_news.title: Oracle EBS hack leaves four corporate giants silent on potential impact
breaking_news.summary:
SecurityWeek reports that four major corporate giants, including Broadcom and Bechtel, remain silent regarding a potential impact from an Oracle EBS hack. The incident suggests a widespread compromise affecting critical enterprise resource planning systems across multiple industries.

The lack of public statements from these Fortune 500 companies indicates the severity of the potential breach and the ongoing investigation into the scope of the attack. Organizations relying on Oracle EBS should monitor for signs of compromise and review their security configurations.
